label: Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping

node.label: Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping
node.definition: Adaptive-compute mechanisms choose how many layers, recurrent steps, or token updates an input receives and may stop when a confidence or budget criterion is met.
node.why_it_matters: They allocate expensive computation to difficult cases rather than applying one fixed depth to every input.
limitations: Routing errors can under-compute difficult inputs, create irregular hardware utilization, and complicate calibration.

dependencies.rationale: Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Gating & multiplicative control.
Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Routing, conditional computation & mixture-of-experts.
In this atlas graph, Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping has a `composes` dependency on Residual, skip & highway connections with `common` strength.

In this atlas graph, Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping has a `uses` dependency on Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives because Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
